title: dicentra formosa subsp. formosa
intro: Dicentra formosa subsp. formosa, also known as the fragrant bleeding heart, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its heart-shaped flowers dangling below arching stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Dicentra formosa subsp. formosa include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote relaxation and well-being.
